zh.lifeSummary: 當Capt. William Tucker於1588年1月7日出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，他的父親John Tucker43歲，母親Alice Pelham17歲。他於1617年與Mary Thompson在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州加斯托結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1624年住在美國維吉尼亞州伊麗莎白 希迪。他去世於1643年之後在愛爾蘭，埋葬在亞特 澤亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Capt. William Tucker于1588年1月7日出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，他的父亲John Tucker43岁，母亲Alice Pelham17岁。他于1617年与Mary Thompson在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州加斯托结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1624年住在美国弗吉尼亚州伊麗莎白 希迪。他去世于1643年之后在爱尔兰，埋葬在亞特 澤亞。

nameMeanings.name: Tucker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name from Middle English tuker(e), toker(e) ‘tucker, fuller’, a derivative of tuken ‘to torment, beat’ (Old English tūcian), for someone who fulled and finished cloth. This name for the occupation was characteristic of the West Country. Compare Fuller and Walker and see also Tuckerman .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tuachair ‘descendant of Tuachar’, a personal name composed of the elements tuath ‘people’ + car ‘dear, beloved’.
Americanized form of Jewish Tocker or Toker (see Tokarz ).

variants.name: Tooker
meanings: English (Devon) and Irish: variant of   Tucker  .

variants.name: Tuckerman
meanings: English: from Middle English tuker(e), toker(e) ‘tucker, fuller’ (see Tucker 1) + -man, either for a tucker, or for his servant or assistant.

variants.name: Fuller
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Swinger
meanings: English: occupational name, perhaps for a fuller, who finished cloth by beating it and stretching it on tenters. Middle English swyngere is not independently recorded but it appears to be an agent derivative of Middle English swingen ‘to beat, strike, punish, afflict (something)’. Compare the synonymous Tucker , an agent derivative of Middle English tuken ‘to torment, beat’. The earliest bearers of the name Swinger were wealthy townsmen.
In some instances probably an Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Schwinger , or German Zwinger, a nickname from Middle High German zwinger ‘oppressor’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: Accession of James I. Surrender of Hugh O'Neill. Enforcement of English Law in Ireland.

The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

Great Catholic-Gaelic rebellion for return of lands, later joined by Old English Catholics in Ireland. Under leadership of Irish chieftain, Rory O'More, conspiracy was formed to seize Dublin and expel the English. English settlers were driven out of Ulster. Catholics hold 59% of land in Ireland.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Capt. William Tucker was born on 7 January 1588, in London, Middlesex, England, his father, John Tucker, was 43 and his mother, Alice Pelham, was 17. He married Mary Thompson in 1617, in Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Elizabeth City, Virginia, United States in 1624. He died after 1643, in Ireland, and was buried in At Sea.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Capt. William Tucker est né le 7 janvier 1588, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, John Tucker, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Alice Pelham, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Mary Thompson en 1617, à Jamestown, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Elizabeth City, Virginie, États-Unis en 1624. Il est décédé après 1643, à Irlande, et a été enterré à En mer.

de.lifeSummary: Als Capt. William Tucker am 7. Januar 1588 in London, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Tucker 43 und seine Mutter Alice Pelham 17. Er hat 1617 in Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America Mary Thompson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1624 hat er in Elizabeth City, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb nach 1643 in Irland und wurde in Auf dem Meer begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Capt. William Tucker è nato il il 7 gennaio 1588 nel London, Middlesex, England, il suo padre, John Tucker, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Alice Pelham, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Mary Thompson nel 1617, nel Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Elizabeth City, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1624. Morì dopo il 1643, nel Irlanda, e fu sepolto a At Sea.

ja.lifeSummary: Capt. William Tuckerが1588年1月7日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父John Tuckerは43歳，母Alice Pelhamは17歳であった。1617年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ジャムストウンでMary Thompsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1624年にはアメリカ合衆国バージニア州エリザベス シトイに住んでいた。彼は1643年以後にアイルランドで死去し，アト セアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Capt. William Tucker(이)가 1588년 1월 7일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Tucker(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Pelham(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1617년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 자메스타운에서 Mary Thompson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1624년도에 미국 버지니아 주 엘리자베스 시티에서 살았다. 그는 1643년 이후 아일랜드에서 사망했으며 앳 시에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Capt. William Tucker nasceu em 7 de janeiro de 1588, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Tucker, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Alice Pelham, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Thompson em 1617, em Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele viveu em Elizabeth City, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América em 1624. Ele faleceu após 1643, em Irlanda, e foi sepultado em Em alto mar.

ru.London St Clement Eastcheap with St Mart: лондон ст клемент эасчип вит ст мартинь оргар, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.St Nicholas Acons, London, London, Engla: лондон ст эдмунд т кинг вит ст ничолас акунс, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.Elizabeth City, Virginia, British Coloni: элизабет сити, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Capt. William Tucker родился 7 января 1588 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени John Tucker было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Pelham, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Thompson в 1617 г. в местности джеместоун, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности элизабет сити, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в 1624 году. Он умер после 1643 г. в местности Ирландия и был похоронен в местности ат сеа.

es.Elizabeth City, Virginia, British Coloni: Elizabeth City, Virginia, British Colonial America
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Capt. William Tucker nació el 7 de enero de 1588, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, John Tucker, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Alice Pelham, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Mary Thompson en 1617, en Jamestown, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en Elizabeth City, Virginia, Estados Unidos en 1624. Murió después de 1643, en Irlanda, y fue sepultado en En el mar.
